patterns.name: ISR_Constraint
patterns.operational_definition: All ISR bodies must complete within a bounded, deterministic execution window with zero blocking calls.
patterns.measurement_proxy: AST-scan detecting vTaskDelay, printf, HAL_Delay, or xSemaphoreTake (non-FromISR variant) inside IRQ_Handler scopes.
patterns.task_conditioned_baseline: WCET of ISR ≤ 5µs on Cortex-M4 @ 168 MHz; escalates to ≤ 1µs for hard real-time audio or motor-control ISRs.
patterns.standards_reference: MISRA C:2025 Rule 8.13 (pointer qualifiers) + MISRA Dir 4.1 (runtime determinism)
patterns.boundary_conditions: Applies exclusively in IRQ_Handler / ISR context. Non-interrupt code may use blocking APIs.
patterns.diagnostic_test: DCCDSchemaGuard AST-scan: reject if blocking token appears in IRQ_Handler subtree.

patterns.name: Memory_Stasis
patterns.operational_definition: No dynamic heap allocation post-main() in continuous execution paths. All task stacks, buffers, and queues declared as static arrays.
patterns.measurement_proxy: Memory-map diff at T+0h vs T+72h; heap watermark via xPortGetFreeHeapSize() and uxTaskGetStackHighWaterMark().
patterns.task_conditioned_baseline: Heap growth post-main() = 0 bytes for devices with >90 day uptime requirement.
patterns.freertos_version: FreeRTOS Kernel v11.1.0 LTS — StaticTask_t / StaticQueue_t APIs
patterns.standards_reference: MISRA C:2025 Rule 21.3 (dynamic allocation prohibited in safety-critical code)
patterns.boundary_conditions: Relaxed only for boot-time initialization before scheduler start. Non-critical data-plane code may use heap2/heap4 with bounded alloc.
patterns.diagnostic_test: malloc/calloc/free call-graph scan post-main-entry. Memory map shows dynamic region frozen.

patterns.name: Concurrency_Lock
patterns.operational_definition: All shared mutable state between RTOS tasks or between ISRs and tasks must be mediated via FreeRTOS queue, mutex, or event-group primitives.
patterns.measurement_proxy: CFDI (Context-Free Dependency Index) scan: flag any global volatile variable read in task context without mutex guard.
patterns.task_conditioned_baseline: Zero unguarded global volatile accesses visible across task boundaries in production builds.
patterns.freertos_version: FreeRTOS Kernel v11.1.0 LTS — xSemaphoreCreateMutex, xQueueCreate, xEventGroupCreate
patterns.standards_reference: MISRA C:2025 Dir 4.7 (volatile access integrity) + CERT C CON43-C
patterns.boundary_conditions: ISR-to-task communication: only *FromISR variants allowed. Mutex not usable from ISR — use binary semaphore or queue.
patterns.diagnostic_test: Static analyzer (e.g., Polyspace, PC-lint Plus) with MISRA C:2025 concurrent-access rule set.

patterns.name: Watchdog_Omnipresence
patterns.operational_definition: Hardware Watchdog Timer (WDT/IWDG) must be configured, enabled, and fed within every idle/main task cycle. Absence = fatal architectural defect.
patterns.measurement_proxy: Code scan: presence of HAL_IWDG_Refresh() / esp_task_wdt_reset() in every task that could block the WDT feed path.
patterns.task_conditioned_baseline: WDT timeout ≤ 1s for safety-critical tasks; ≤ 8s for non-critical telemetry.
patterns.standards_reference: ESP-IDF v6.0 esp_task_wdt API; STM32 HAL IWDG API; Zephyr watchdog subsystem (wdt_feed)
patterns.boundary_conditions: Not required in test/debug build configurations where reset would obscure debugging. Must be re-enabled for release builds.
patterns.diagnostic_test: Build artifact analysis: strip debug flags, verify WDT init + feed in all task while(1) loops.

patterns.name: Volatile_Mandate
patterns.operational_definition: Any C variable mutated inside an ISR and read in non-ISR context MUST be declared volatile to prevent compiler reordering/caching.
patterns.measurement_proxy: AST-scan: variables assigned within IRQ_Handler scope cross-referenced against non-ISR read sites; missing volatile qualifier flagged.
patterns.task_conditioned_baseline: 100% volatile compliance on ISR-shared variables for MISRA C:2025 required-rule conformance.
patterns.standards_reference: MISRA C:2025 Rule 8.13; ISO C11 §6.7.3 volatile semantics; ARM Cortex-M Architecture TRM §B3.5
patterns.boundary_conditions: Applies to plain C. In Embedded Rust, replace volatile with core::sync::atomic (AtomicU32, AtomicBool) with Ordering::SeqCst.
patterns.diagnostic_test: Compile with -Wall -Wextra -Werror; also GCC -Wsuggest-attribute=const flags potential volatile misuse.

patterns.name: OTA_Dual_Bank
patterns.operational_definition: Over-The-Air update architecture must employ dual-bank flash layout with atomic commit and automatic rollback on boot failure.
patterns.measurement_proxy: Linker script analysis: two independent OTA partition slots; boot counter in NVS; rollback logic in bootloader.
patterns.task_conditioned_baseline: OTA success rate ≥ 99.95% across fleet; failed update auto-reverts within 3 boot attempts.
patterns.standards_reference: ESP-IDF v6.0 OTA API (esp_ota_ops.h); MCUboot for Zephyr; STM32 custom dual-bank HAL
patterns.boundary_conditions: Requires ≥ 2× application flash size. Single-bank MCUs require external SPI flash staging.
patterns.diagnostic_test: HIL test: interrupt power during OTA write at 50%, 75%, 99% completion; verify rollback on next boot.
